items.rationale: Sees restaking as unbundling cryptoeconomic security from the EVM, enabling '100x faster innovation' on consensus, VMs, oracles, bridges without spinning up new L1s — 'the verifiable cloud.'

citations.quote: Today, we're announcing that we're leading @eigencloud's Series B.
EigenLayer is the verifiable cloud. Massive leap forward today toward the realization of that vision.

items.rationale: Champions ZK as unlocking scalability and new design space; celebrated Lasso/Jolt as a 'lookup singularity' and shipped Jolt Alpha as the fastest open-source zkVM.

citations.quote: Today we're releasing Jolt Alpha ⚡️ ✅ Fastest zkVM in the world.
Lasso and Jolt are groundbreaking. Lasso is already 10x faster than Halo2's lookup argument

items.rationale: Believes L2s' core value is unlocking permissionless innovation atop Ethereum without fragmenting network effects; backs the rollup-centric roadmap toward millions of rollups.

citations.quote: The most important benefit of L2s is not performance. It is that it unlocks permissionless innovation on top of Ethereum.
Ethereum's scalability end-game is all about rollups—potentially thousands or even millions of rollups

items.rationale: Sees fragmentation as the biggest risk of a rollup future; excited by decoupling security from liveness (LayerZero v2) and coordinating rollup sequencers (Espresso).

citations.quote: @LayerZero_Labs just launched v2 💥 It radically improves... by cleanly decoupling security from liveness.
Today, we're announcing that we're leading @EspressoSys' Series B ☕

items.rationale: Argues crypto can't reach mainstream adoption without privacy; excited by private smart contracts (Aztec) and verifiable-yet-confidential computation (Aleo).

citations.quote: It's hard to imagine crypto ever reaching mainstream adoption without privacy.
Aleo network is live 🔥 ... a blockchain that is privacy-preserving while still being fully verifiable.

items.rationale: Believes in decentralized information markets for contributing and consuming knowledge; this is his most recent area of public enthusiasm (Murmur, following earlier Freatic bet).
items.age_note: Most recent sector signal (Dec 2024); consistent with 2023-09 Freatic thesis

citations.quote: Murmur Protocol: A marketplace for alpha. Let's go 🔥
Freatic wanted to build generalized information markets for anyone to contribute or consume knowledge.

items.rationale: Interested in decentralizing AI infrastructure, zkML, and proof-of-humanity; sees this as a major emerging intersection.

citations.quote: Had a great time chatting with @danboneh and @smc90 about the intersection of AI and crypto.
NEAR is emerging as the epicenter for AI x crypto.

items.rationale: His explicit forward-looking call for 2025 — expects verification of code correctness to grow in importance, aligning with his ZK/verifiability focus.
items.age_note: Most recent statement in corpus (2024-12-20); single forward prediction

citations.quote: 2025 prediction: Formal verification will become increasingly important

items.rationale: Backs user-owned, permissionless messaging (XMTP) as core web3 infrastructure controlled by users not tech giants.
items.age_note: Last addressed mid-2023; not restated since, treat as aging

citations.quote: XMTP is emerging as a core messaging protocol for web3 — it is permissionless, open source

items.rationale: Excited about removing intermediaries between artists and fans via artist-owned smart contracts (Sound.xyz).
items.age_note: Strong in mid-2023 (led Series A); no fresh mentions since Sept 2023, conviction discounted for age

citations.quote: Sound is music without the middleman; a web3 music platform that is powering the next generation of artists

items.rationale: Views meaningless speculation as the primary force holding the space back; rejects the 'we need the casino to bootstrap' argument as an excuse for bad behavior.

citations.quote: We don't need the casino. We never did. It's the primary force holding the space back.
Underexplored — token lockups, retroactive funding... rewards for verifiable work, on-chain reputation

items.rationale: Frames the real existential threat as halting innovation; criticizes web2 monopolies and 'technically illiterate bureaucrats' constraining what can be built.

citations.quote: Our greatest existential risk is the risk of stagnation and atrophy.
We need to be taking more risks on new tech, not less.

items.rationale: Acknowledges the biggest disadvantage of millions of rollups is loss of composability/state synchronization across separate chains — a problem his investments aim to solve.

citations.quote: The biggest disadvantage of a rollup-centric future is the fragmentation of the Ethereum ecosystem.

items.rationale: Notes the modularity paradox — separating components (e.g., distinct DA chains) can add single points of failure rather than redundancy, and creates hard-to-change interface constraints.

citations.quote: It depends on whether modularity adds redundancy... or adds more single points of failure (as with separate blockchains for DA, etc).

items.rationale: Led the EigenLayer Series B; sees it as a two-sided marketplace for raw security that unlocks 100x faster open innovation on Ethereum.

citations.quote: By further unbundling cryptoeconomic security from the EVM, EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation

items.rationale: Praises Aleo's combination of confidentiality and scalability via ZK, extending the application design space beyond transparent chains.

citations.quote: Aleo's breakthrough is to use cutting edge cryptography in the form of zero knowledge proofs to create a blockchain that is privacy-preserving

items.rationale: Singled out as the emerging center of gravity for AI x crypto and for aligning with the modular Ethereum stack.
items.age_note: Stated once (May 2024); not restated, treat as aging

citations.quote: NEAR is emerging as the epicenter for AI x crypto.

items.rationale: Implicit base-layer conviction throughout — frames Ethereum as the 'bedrock of security' and most secure programmable blockchain on which all his theses build.
items.age_note: Inferred from repeated Ethereum-centric framing; never a direct token call

items.rationale: Explicitly disparages 'tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff' as a drag on the space.

citations.quote: we don't need tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff.

risk_regime.rationale: Yahya is structurally and aggressively pro-innovation, arguing stagnation is the true existential risk and that the space should take MORE technology risk. His risk-on posture is firmly tilted toward fundamental infrastructure (restaking, ZK, interop, privacy, verifiability) and explicitly OFF speculative/degen activity — he separates 'building new things' from 'a degenerate casino.' Most recent signals (Murmur alpha markets, formal verification) reinforce a builder-oriented, long-horizon bullishness.

notes: Corpus is heavily qualitative/thesis-driven with few direct token calls; token entries are largely protocols/companies he led investments into (EigenLayer, Aleo, Espresso, LayerZero, Sound, XMTP) rather than trading views. Strongest and most durable convictions are restaking, ZK/zkVMs, and modular-Ethereum interoperability — all restated multiple times across 2023-2024. Privacy and AI x crypto are real but less frequently voiced. Music (Sound) and messaging (XMTP) were prominent in mid-2023 but went quiet, so conviction is age-discounted. The single most recent datapoints (2024-12-19/20) are the Murmur alpha-market endorsement and a 2025 formal-verification prediction, both consistent with his verifiability/anti-speculation worldview. No stance reversals detected; views are remarkably consistent over the 18-month span — primarily additive rather than contradictory.
